content: Fitts's Law: time-to-acquire-target increases as target size decreases and/or distance increases; small targets measurably increase user error rate. Common accessibility/UX minimum target-size guidance clusters around 24-44px depending on platform (WCAG minimum 24x24 CSS px, Apple iOS 44x44pt, Android Material 48dp), with additional minimum spacing (~8-10px) recommended between adjacent small targets to prevent mis-hits. For mouse-driven precision interfaces (vs touch), the same principle applies at a smaller absolute scale, but a visually tiny symbol should still have a larger invisible hit-region than its rendered size.
